{{Quote|Bhat Jul was a Nikto and, like a Tusken, he understood the desert. To become one with the desert was, for him, a way to honor the life that he drew from it.|[[Darth Krayt|A'Sharad Hett]]|<ref name="Republic 59">[[Republic 59|''Republic'' 59]]</ref>}}
'''Bhat Jul''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Kajain'sa'Nikto/Legends|Kajain'sa'Nikto]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] as a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Jedi Commander/Legends|Commander]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. The [[Nikto/Legends|Nikto]] was [[Padawan/Legends|apprenticed]] to [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Darth Krayt|A'Sharad Hett]] at the [[Time/Legends|time]].
Unfortunately, during an [[Battle of Aargonar (Clone Wars)|early deployment]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Aargonar/Legends|Aargonar]], Bhat Jul spent his [[Death/Legends|last moments]] in [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker's]] [[Limb/Legends|arms]] as he suffered from explosion-induced injuries. The young Jedi was left to be [[Burial/Legends|covered]] in the sand to the bidding of his [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]], Hett, who was a [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raider]] who believed the act would honor the fallen Nikto's [[The Force/Legends|life]].

==Personality and traits==
Sensitive to [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], Bhat Jul was chosen as a Padawan learner by [[Darth Krayt|A'Sharad Hett]].

==Behind the scenes==
Bhat Jul appears in the 58th and 59th parts from the Star Wars Republic comic series released on [[December 17]] [[2003]] and [[December 31]] [[2003]] by [[Dark Horse Comics]].
